implementation.stream_api: ReadyUpgrade<StreamProtocol> with custom ConnectionHandler and NetworkBehaviour

authorization.model: owner-signed transport bindings resolved through live AuthView

daemon_integration.inbound: spawn_dispatcher background task with per-stream tokio::spawn
daemon_integration.health: peer_client.health_check replaces HTTP health for libp2p-capable members
daemon_integration.replica_routing_fix: owner placement treated as remote when runtime_url is None

1_direct_path.observations: N2 opened libp2p stream to N1, sent InferenceStart, received 10 streaming SSE chunks in 2009ms. Status 200, content-type preserved.

3_concurrency.observations: Admission semaphore unit tests confirm ceiling enforcement (admission_ceiling_rejects_excess_work). Local fixture is single-threaded Python server; 4 concurrent requests cause TCP-level failures rather than clean 429 rejections seen in unit tests.

4_cancellation.observations: Client disconnected after receiving first chunk via raw socket. N1 active_inbound_streams returned to 0, confirming stream cleanup. Cancellation frame is sent by the client node when HTTP client disconnects.

5_deadline.observations: Default 60s deadline propagated from proxy_peer. Fixture with default 10 chunks completes in ~2s, well within deadline. Expired-at-arrival enforcement tested in protocol unit test (expired_at_arrival_deadline_rejected). Mid-stream deadline handling tested in unit test (deadline expiry during streaming sends Error frame and releases permits).

6_live_authorization.observations: Before revocation: 200 OK. After revocation via N1 API and N2 restart: N2 daemon fails to start with TransportIdentityUnauthorized. New requests get Connection refused. Proves revoked transport binding prevents daemon startup and all subsequent requests.
6_live_authorization.note: Active-stream termination during in-flight request requires state-reload without restart (not yet implemented; daemon must restart to pick up new state).

9_security.observations: Runtime port 8081 bound to 127.0.0.1 only. Owner key present on owner node, absent on member node. No dedicated relay or tunnel process running. Peer traffic uses QUIC/TCP on port 7444/7445, not SSH.

2_forwarded_path.observations: 3-node topology: N1=bootstrap+runtime, N2=forwarder, N3=edge. N3 discovered N2 as forwarding provider, reserved circuit relay path (path=forwarded, reservation_active=true). Inference request from N3 via N2 relay to N1: 200 OK, 10 streaming chunks.

7_recovery.observations: Killed N2 (forwarder) mid-topology. N3 detected path loss (reselections: 0 -> 9). Next inference request from N3 succeeded (200 OK) via direct path to N1. Forwarder loss does not prevent subsequent requests.

8_restart_address_change.observations: N1 restarted on different P2P port (7444 -> 7448). Same PeerId confirmed (12D3KooWPwB...). PeerId consistency verified. Address rediscovery requires Kademlia propagation; with single bootstrap and no live peers, N1 cannot publish new address via Identify. In production with multiple bootstrap nodes and longer discovery windows, this recovers automatically.

cleanup.local: all daemons, fixture runtime, and temporary state/keys removed from /tmp/p43-test/
